description_long: This object is a flat, irregularly shaped slab of stone with a roughly pentagonal outline and a thin profile. Its surface is predominantly gray, with subtle variations in tone and a slightly rough, matte finish. The edges are uneven and chipped, revealing a layered internal structure typical of sedimentary rock. The sides show some color variation, with hints of brown and beige, suggesting natural mineral inclusions or weathering. The stone appears solid and dense, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. Its size and shape make it suitable for use as a paving stone or stepping stone in urban landscaping.
description: A flat, irregular gray stone slab with rough edges and a matte, slightly textured surface. Subtle color variations and layered sides suggest natural weathering.
description_view_0: This view highlights the stone's thin profile and one of its more angular, chipped edges, with a visible beige layer along the side.
description_view_1: The stone appears broader and flatter from this angle, with a prominent straight edge and a clear view of the layered side structure.
description_view_2: This perspective shows a darker, rougher edge and emphasizes the stone's irregular, polygonal shape.
description_view_3: The stone looks widest and flattest here, with the surface texture and subtle color variations most visible.
attribute_placement: set flat on the ground, often as part of a paved surface or stepping stone arrangement
attribute_pattern: subtle natural layering and mottling, with no artificial carvings or regular patterns
attribute_weathering: slightly rough and matte, with minor edge chipping and some color variation indicating mild weathering
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: blends naturally with adjacent paving stones or ground cover, may be partially surrounded by soil or vegetation
